test_cases.description: Note-taking app — Inkwell — science podcast, warm nerdy host, personal endorsement, pre-roll + mid-roll
test_cases.input_summary: B2B-adjacent software, specific audience fit (research-intensive podcast listeners), personal endorsement confirmed, promo code provided, two placement versions requested
test_cases.output_summary: Pre-roll at 80 words is clean and direct; mid-roll at 150 words opens with transition, uses specific personal usage story, restates code once clearly at end; compliance note added flagging personal endorsement verification requirement

test_cases.description: Online mattress brand — health/wellness podcast, upbeat host, no personal endorsement, mid-roll only
test_cases.input_summary: DTC consumer product, wellness podcast audience, no personal endorsement (host hasn't used it), host required claims provided verbatim by brand
test_cases.output_summary: Script avoids endorsement language while remaining warm; required brand claims integrated naturally; one required phrase flagged as slightly awkward in spoken form with alternative offered

test_cases.description: Financial services tool — freelance business podcast, dry professional host, high compliance requirements
test_cases.input_summary: Regulated category (financial product), compliance disclaimers required, host tone is professional and unsentimental
test_cases.output_summary: Ad copy is clean and accurate; compliance note flags that financial product ads require specific disclaimers per jurisdiction; delivery note recommends host maintain dry register and avoid any implied financial advice

notes: Highest-scoring podcast skill in this batch. The skill excels when a personal endorsement angle is available — the specificity of 'I use this for X' copy is noticeably stronger than non-endorsement versions. Compliance flagging is a key professionalism differentiator.
